title: Rest, consolidation, and preview for Linux ramp-up and first VM build
secondary_exam: Bridge to CompTIA Network+ (N10-009) and CompTIA Security+ (SY0-701)
week_focus: Linux ramp-up and first VM build
deliverable: Ubuntu VM + Linux command cheat sheet
checkpoint: Can navigate Linux comfortably enough to start labs and tooling.
plain_language_intro: Today is a light consolidation day for linux ramp-up and first vm build.
mini_lecture_markdown:
Today is a rest day for Linux ramp-up and first VM build.

Keep the week lightly in view, but do not turn the day into a full study session. Next, the roadmap moves into networking foundations: models, media, devices, and topologies.

The main goal is to preserve continuity without adding another heavy block of material.

A short preview of the next week is enough to keep continuity without turning the day back into a long session.

The rest day exists to preserve momentum and clarity rather than add another dense topic.
exam_relevance_markdown: Rest days matter indirectly for CompTIA A+ Core 2 (220-1202) because spaced retrieval and reduced overload help you keep earlier concepts available when scenario questions combine multiple topics.
key_distinctions: Recognition vs diagnosis — knowing the term name is not enough; know what evidence would make you pick it in a scenario.
mental_model_markdown: Picture the week as a simple chain of concept, mechanism, evidence, and decision.
self_check_prompts: Walk through today's objective in sequence and say which step would fail first if the system were misconfigured.
Connect today's glossary terms to this week's deliverable: what part of the artifact proves that you understand the mechanism and not just the label?
What idea from this week would still confuse you if it appeared inside a short scenario tomorrow?
quick_recap: Today keeps linux ramp-up and first vm build organized without adding more heavy content.
